title: Simple Changing Table
summary: An open two-shelf table with a removable topper tray that contains the changing pad — take the topper off later and it's just a plain shelf unit.
description:
The topper is the whole idea here: a shallow-walled tray that sits on top of the table and keeps a changing pad from sliding around, attached from underneath with screws only — no glue — specifically so it can be removed once diaper-changing days are over. What's left underneath is a completely ordinary two-shelf table, which is the point: this piece is meant to outlive its original job.

Miter the edge trim corners at 45° for a clean, gap-free look around the top — the plan doesn't give fixed dimensions for this trim, so cut each piece to fit as you go rather than trying to pre-cut from a cut list.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood into shelf-width strips.

materials.name: 3/4" MDF or hardwood plywood, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Ripped into 15-1/2"-wide strips for the shelves.

steps.title: Build the end assemblies
steps.body: Build the two end assemblies from the legs and end trim. Use 2" screws and glue, or attach the end trim from the insides with a pocket-hole jig if you have one.

steps.title: Attach the shelves
steps.body: Fasten the shelves to the end assemblies with 2" screws and glue, or pocket holes driven from the underside of the shelves. Keep the top edge of each shelf flush.

steps.title: Trim the shelf sides
steps.body: Trim out the sides of both shelves with 2" screws and glue, fastening into the legs from the outside for extra support — or use pocket holes.

steps.title: Add the top edge trim
steps.body: Fasten the top edge trim around the top of the table with 1-1/4" nails and glue, mitering the corners at 45° for a clean edge. Cut each piece to fit as you go.

steps.title: Build the topper sides
steps.body: Attach the topper side trim to the topper base, and to the end trim installed in the previous step, using glue and 2" nails or screws.

steps.title: Add the topper partition
steps.body: Attach the partition pieces to the bottom of the topper and to the side/end trim, using glue.

steps.title: Attach the topper
steps.body: From underneath the changing table, attach the topper with 1-1/4" screws only — skip the glue here if you want the option to remove the topper later and use the table as a plain shelf unit.

images.alt: A simple wood changing table with a removable topper tray
